cases.name: create_success
cases.conclusion: stdout is the created box ID

cases.name: inspect_configured
cases.conclusion: inspect JSON is an array; Status and State.Status were both configured

cases.name: inspect_missing
cases.conclusion: missing inspect should map to sandbox.ErrNotFound

cases.name: exec_missing
cases.conclusion: missing exec uses different capitalization: No such box

cases.name: rm_force_configured
cases.conclusion: successful remove stdout is the box name

cases.name: start_pull_failure
cases.conclusion: failed start can remove the DB record and leave an orphaned box directory

cases.name: start_proxy_auth_failure
cases.conclusion: proxy rerun reached Docker Hub but failed on bootstrap debian:bookworm-slim manifest authorization

cases.name: start_after_docker_login_auth_failure

environment.docker_login: docker login docker.io succeeded before this run

cases.conclusion: Docker CLI login did not unblock BoxLite bootstrap debian:bookworm-slim manifest pull

cases.name: start_success_manual_combined
cases.conclusion: manual retry succeeded after layer download retries; stream-split stdout/stderr still needs capture

cases.name: detached_create_success
cases.conclusion: stdout is the created detached box ID

cases.name: detached_start_success
cases.stderr_note: large image-pull and VM startup logs omitted from per-case fixture
cases.conclusion: detached start stdout is the box name and leaves the box running

cases.name: detached_inspect_running
cases.conclusion: Status and State.Status are running; State.Running is true

cases.name: detached_exec_ok
cases.stderr_note: runtime INFO logs plus a seccomp warning
cases.conclusion: command stdout is forwarded and exit code is zero

cases.name: detached_exec_exit7
cases.conclusion: boxlite preserves the command exit code; command stderr is mixed with runtime stderr

cases.name: detached_stop_success
cases.conclusion: successful stop stdout is the box name

cases.name: detached_inspect_stopped
cases.conclusion: Status and State.Status are stopped; State.Running is false

cases.name: context_kill
cases.conclusion: SIGTERM kill is visible as shell exit code 143; Go CommandContext should still surface ctx cancellation
